name: Grilled Garlicky Skewers with Tomatillo Salsa
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the 5 cloves of garlic to a coarse puree in a mortar and pestle.
2. Add olive oil and grind together until smooth. Scrape into a mixing bowl and whisk with the juice of 2 1/2 limes until well combined.
3. (Alternative: If using a blender, blend garlic and oil together, then pulse with lime juice.)
4. Cut pork and beef into thin slices about 1/4 to 1/2 inch thick.
5. Dust the meat liberally with salt, pepper, red pepper flakes, and oregano.
6. Dip each pork slice one at a time in garlic paste and place in a plastic storage bag. Repeat with beef slices and another plastic bag.
7. Seal bags tightly and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
8. Broil tomatillos in a single layer about 1 inch from the heat source until the tops begin to blacken and blister.
9. Remove from broiler, cut in half, and puree with the remaining garlic clove and juice of 1/2 lime in a blender until smooth.
10. Season with salt to taste and add more lime juice if needed. Pour into a serving bowl and stir in cilantro.
11. Thread meat onto skewers and grill over very hot fire until browned and crisp on the outside but still moist inside, about 7 minutes on the first side and 5 minutes on the second.
12. Place skewers on a platter and serve with tomatillo salsa.
[8 servings. Each serving: 255 calories; 129 mg sodium; 68 mg cholesterol; 15 grams fat; 5 grams carbohydrates; 24 grams protein; 0.04 gram fiber.]
